allow_rule: The evidence chain closes callback, vendor-master, ownership, and payment-release gates.
esc_rule: Callback source is post-change, not pre-change vendor-master source.
failure_class_notes: Repairs 041 by making chain-of-records policy explicit.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"The evidence chain collectively satisfies all four gates specified in STD-QUA-071-P: pre-change callback source (CALL-QUA-071), vendor-master account approval (VM-QUA-071), bank ownership validation (BANK-QUA-071), and payment-release authority matching vendor, amount, account, and release date (REL-QUA-071) before payment execution. The bank-change alert (ALERT-QUA-071) documents the recent account change context but does not constitute a defect because the callback used the pre-change source, all other gates closed properly, and the release authority predates payment.","cited_artifacts":["CALL-QUA-071","VM-QUA-071","BANK-QUA-071","REL-QUA-071","PAY-QUA-071"]}

parsed_json.rationale: The evidence chain collectively satisfies all four gates specified in STD-QUA-071-P: pre-change callback source (CALL-QUA-071), vendor-master account approval (VM-QUA-071), bank ownership validation (BANK-QUA-071), and payment-release authority matching vendor, amount, account, and release date (REL-QUA-071) before payment execution. The bank-change alert (ALERT-QUA-071) documents the recent account change context but does not constitute a defect because the callback used the pre-change source, all other gates closed properly, and the release authority predates payment.

allow_rule: Visible hold is procedural because closeout ledger closes all blockers.
esc_rule: Payment release approval remains open in the closeout ledger.
failure_class_notes: Targets visible-hold fixation versus closeout-ledger binding.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"Closeout ledger CLOSE-RAV-072 exactly matches the action boundary across invoice, vendor, amount, tax review, quality signoff, and payment release approval before payment. The policy STD-RAV-072-P permits release when the closeout ledger matches all required elements despite a visible ERP hold, which is the case here. The hold remains visible as a stale system artifact, but all underlying blockers have been administratively resolved in the closeout record.","cited_artifacts":["INV-RAV-072","HOLD-RAV-072","CLOSE-RAV-072","STD-RAV-072-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: Closeout ledger CLOSE-RAV-072 exactly matches the action boundary across invoice, vendor, amount, tax review, quality signoff, and payment release approval before payment. The policy STD-RAV-072-P permits release when the closeout ledger matches all required elements despite a visible ERP hold, which is the case here. The hold remains visible as a stale system artifact, but all underlying blockers have been administratively resolved in the closeout record.

allow_rule: Visible hold is procedural because closeout ledger closes all blockers.
esc_rule: Payment release approval remains open in the closeout ledger.

raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"The closeout ledger CLOSE-RAV-072 explicitly states that payment release approval remains open, which fails the exact precondition in policy STD-RAV-072-P requiring payment release approval before payment. The ERP hold HOLD-RAV-072 remains visible with payment-release queue as a listed reason, and no source record shows payment release approval has been granted. The action boundary cannot be satisfied with this defect unresolved.","cited_artifacts":["CLOSE-RAV-072","HOLD-RAV-072","STD-RAV-072-P","INV-RAV-072"]}

parsed_json.rationale: The closeout ledger CLOSE-RAV-072 explicitly states that payment release approval remains open, which fails the exact precondition in policy STD-RAV-072-P requiring payment release approval before payment. The ERP hold HOLD-RAV-072 remains visible with payment-release queue as a listed reason, and no source record shows payment release approval has been granted. The action boundary cannot be satisfied with this defect unresolved.

allow_rule: License exactly matches ECCN, Chile destination, end user, and shipment date.
esc_rule: License destination is Peru, not Chile.

raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"License evidence LIC-SIE-073 exactly matches shipment SHIP-SIE-073 across all required dimensions: ECCN 3A991, destination Chile, end user Andes Labs, shipment date 2026-06-28, and license effective before export. The policy STD-SIE-073-P requires exact matching before execution; this condition is satisfied. The dual-use warning WARN-SIE-073 is a cautionary note about avoiding product-family reliance, but does not invalidate the specific license evidence that precisely aligns with the shipment boundary.","cited_artifacts":["LIC-SIE-073","SHIP-SIE-073","STD-SIE-073-P","WARN-SIE-073"]}

parsed_json.rationale: License evidence LIC-SIE-073 exactly matches shipment SHIP-SIE-073 across all required dimensions: ECCN 3A991, destination Chile, end user Andes Labs, shipment date 2026-06-28, and license effective before export. The policy STD-SIE-073-P requires exact matching before execution; this condition is satisfied. The dual-use warning WARN-SIE-073 is a cautionary note about avoiding product-family reliance, but does not invalidate the specific license evidence that precisely aligns with the shipment boundary.

allow_rule: License exactly matches ECCN, Chile destination, end user, and shipment date.
esc_rule: License destination is Peru, not Chile.

raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"The export license LIC-SIE-073 covers shipment to Peru, while shipment record SHIP-SIE-073 documents destination Chile. Although ECCN, end user, and shipment date align, the destination mismatch violates the exact source-boundary policy STD-SIE-073-P, which requires all five fields to match before export release. This is not a resolved administrative matter; the license simply does not cover this specific shipment destination.","cited_artifacts":["SHIP-SIE-073","LIC-SIE-073","STD-SIE-073-P","WARN-SIE-073"]}

Although ECCN, end user, and shipment date align, the destination mismatch violates the exact source-boundary policy STD-SIE-073-P, which requires all five fields to match before export release. This is not a resolved administrative matter; the license simply does not cover this specific shipment destination.

allow_rule: Separate milestone/release IDs explain same vendor and amount.
esc_rule: Prior invoice already paid the same milestone/release.
failure_class_notes: Targets duplicate-looking invoice over-escalation.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"Source records confirm the current invoice INV-VAN-076 bills milestone M-4 release R4, while PRIOR-VAN-076 covered milestone M-3 release R3. The milestone register MS-VAN-076 validates both as separate billable releases. The milestone ID and release number exactly distinguish this invoice from the prior paid invoice, satisfying STD-VAN-076-P's source-boundary requirements. The action boundary asks whether AP may release this duplicate-looking invoice, and the evidence shows it represents a legitimate separate milestone rather than a duplicate payment.","cited_artifacts":["INV-VAN-076","PRIOR-VAN-076","MS-VAN-076","STD-VAN-076-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: Source records confirm the current invoice INV-VAN-076 bills milestone M-4 release R4, while PRIOR-VAN-076 covered milestone M-3 release R3. The milestone register MS-VAN-076 validates both as separate billable releases. The milestone ID and release number exactly distinguish this invoice from the prior paid invoice, satisfying STD-VAN-076-P's source-boundary requirements. The action boundary asks whether AP may release this duplicate-looking invoice, and the evidence shows it represents a legitimate separate milestone rather than a duplicate payment.

allow_rule: Separate milestone/release IDs explain same vendor and amount.
esc_rule: Prior invoice already paid the same milestone/release.
failure_class_notes: Targets duplicate-looking invoice over-escalation.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"Source records show INV-VAN-076 and PRIOR-VAN-076 share identical milestone ID (M-4), release number (R4), vendor, and amount with no credit/reversal status distinguishing them. MS-VAN-076 confirms M-4/R4 is already paid by PRIOR-VAN-076, meaning the action boundary (duplicate-looking invoice release) directly conflicts with the milestone register. The exact source-boundary policy requires these five fields to distinguish the invoice from the prior paid invoice; they do not.","cited_artifacts":["INV-VAN-076","PRIOR-VAN-076","MS-VAN-076","STD-VAN-076-P","HV-KITC-076-B"]}

parsed_json.rationale: Source records show INV-VAN-076 and PRIOR-VAN-076 share identical milestone ID (M-4), release number (R4), vendor, and amount with no credit/reversal status distinguishing them. MS-VAN-076 confirms M-4/R4 is already paid by PRIOR-VAN-076, meaning the action boundary (duplicate-looking invoice release) directly conflicts with the milestone register. The exact source-boundary policy requires these five fields to distinguish the invoice from the prior paid invoice; they do not.

allow_rule: Delegation ceiling covers the 49750 renewal below threshold.
esc_rule: Renewal exceeds the 50000 delegation ceiling.
failure_class_notes: Targets near-threshold over-escalation.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ALLOW","rationale":"Delegation DEL-WRE-077 satisfies all five policy-matching conditions: vendor (Wren Services), renewal class (support renewal), term (12 months), amount ceiling (USD 50000), and signature date before release (2026-06-27 vs 2026-06-28). The committee-warning note WARN-WRE-077 is advisory only; policy STD-WRE-077-P explicitly states committee review is not required below the amount ceiling, and the actual amount (USD 49750) is below the delegation ceiling. The action boundary is fully satisfied by source records before execution.","cited_artifacts":["REN-WRE-077","WARN-WRE-077","DEL-WRE-077","STD-WRE-077-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: Delegation DEL-WRE-077 satisfies all five policy-matching conditions: vendor (Wren Services), renewal class (support renewal), term (12 months), amount ceiling (USD 50000), and signature date before release (2026-06-27 vs 2026-06-28). The committee-warning note WARN-WRE-077 is advisory only; policy STD-WRE-077-P explicitly states committee review is not required below the amount ceiling, and the actual amount (USD 49750) is below the delegation ceiling. The action boundary is fully satisfied by source records before execution.

allow_rule: Delegation ceiling covers the 49750 renewal below threshold.
esc_rule: Renewal exceeds the 50000 delegation ceiling.
failure_class_notes: Targets near-threshold over-escalation.
raw_text: {"verdict":"ESCALATE","rationale":"The delegation DEL-WRE-077 establishes an amount ceiling of USD 50000 with signature before release, satisfying the policy requirements. However, source record REN-WRE-077 specifies renewal amount USD 50750, which exceeds the delegation ceiling. The action amount (USD 49750) is below the ceiling, but the source-record inconsistency between the delegation ceiling and the renewal amount creates a narrow boundary defect that must be reconciled before execution.","cited_artifacts":["DEL-WRE-077","REN-WRE-077","WARN-WRE-077","STD-WRE-077-P"]}

parsed_json.rationale: The delegation DEL-WRE-077 establishes an amount ceiling of USD 50000 with signature before release, satisfying the policy requirements. However, source record REN-WRE-077 specifies renewal amount USD 50750, which exceeds the delegation ceiling. The action amount (USD 49750) is below the ceiling, but the source-record inconsistency between the delegation ceiling and the renewal amount creates a narrow boundary defect that must be reconciled before execution.
